title: Cosmarium autumnale
intro: Cosmarium autumnale is a species of green alga, specifically a type of desmid, which is a small, freshwater plant belonging to the class Zygnematophyceae. Research suggests that Cosmarium autumnale may have several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which could potentially help to protect against cell damage and reduce inflammation in the body. The bioactive constituents of Cosmarium autumnale include various pigments such as chlorophyll, carotenoids, and flavonoids, as well as other compounds like glycosides and alkaloids. In traditional herbal medicine, Cosmarium autumnale is often used to prepare infusions, tinctures, and extracts, which are valued for their potential therapeutic properties, including their ability to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and stress.
taxonomy: Cosmarium autumnale belongs to the genus Cosmarium, which is a group of small, freshwater green algae. It is classified as a member of the family Desmidiaceae, a family of green algae characterized by their divided cells. Cosmarium autumnale is further classified as a species within the genus Cosmarium, which contains many other species of green algae. The classification of Cosmarium autumnale is as follows: Kingdom: Chromista, Phylum: Charophyta, Class: Conjugatophyceae, Order: Desmidiales, Family: Desmidiaceae, Genus: Cosmarium. This classification helps to identify and distinguish Cosmarium autumnale from other species of green algae.
